heroHeadline: Haitian Diaspora Rallies in North Miami Eve of SCOTUS TPS Arguments April 27; GSF Advance Force ~545 Continues Operations
heroSubtitle: On April 26, 2026, Haitian diaspora organizations including FANM, the Haitian Bridge Alliance, and the ACLU held a TPS advocacy rally in North Miami — one day before US Supreme Court oral arguments in Noem v. Doe / Trump v. Miot (April 27), the consolidated cases that will determine TPS legal status for approximately 350,000 Haitians. A ruling is expected by late June or early July 2026 and will be determinative if congressional action fails: Senate Republicans pledged to block the House-passed TPS extension bill (H.R. 1689, 224–204, April 16), and Rep. Clyde (R-GA) introduced legislation on April 23 to repeal TPS entirely. On the security front, the Gang Suppression Force advance contingent of ~545 personnel (400 Chadian, 70 Salvadoran, 75 Guatemalan) continues joint operations with the Haitian National Police in the 'early phase of establishment,' with full 5,500-member strength expected fall–end 2026. GSF pledges exceeded the authorized ceiling ($200M+ pledged by 13 states, $59M disbursed, Qatar pledging $30M) following the April 23 UNSC briefing. The WFP April 2026 Country Brief confirmed 5.8 million Haitians (52%) face IPC Phase 3+ food insecurity through June 2026, with the 2026 HRP only ~20% funded ($172M of $880M needed) and USAID cuts deepening the gap.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN BINUH, OHCHR, OCHA, IOM, Reuters, AP, ABC News, Miami Herald, Al Jazeera, France 24, Le Nouvelliste, ACLED, ICG, HRW, WFP, Haitian Times, Jamaica Observer, Jamaica Gleaner, RNDDH, PassBlue, Amnesty International, IMF, HaitiLibre, ICI Haiti, JURIST, International Rescue Committee, NPR, NBC News, Capital FM Kenya, Dominican Today, Security Council Report, UN News, Xinhua, Haiti Sun, SCOTUSblog, Senate Democratic Leadership, WLRN / NPR South Florida, FANM, Haitian Bridge Alliance, ACLU
